---
title: "Veille arXiv"
date: today
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD"
---

# Veille arXiv

## Catégories surveillées

| Catégorie arXiv | Description | Fréquence de scan |
|-----------------|-------------|-------------------|
| `cs.AI` | Artificial Intelligence | Quotidien |
| `cs.CL` | Computation and Language (NLP) | Quotidien |
| `cs.LG` | Machine Learning | Quotidien |
| `cs.MA` | Multi-Agent Systems | Quotidien |
| `cs.SE` | Software Engineering | Quotidien |
| `stat.ML` | Machine Learning (Stats) | Quotidien |

## Filtres de pertinence

Le script `scripts/veille_arxiv.py` filtre les papers selon :

| Filtre | Mots-clés | Action |
|--------|-----------|--------|
| Agentic | `agent`, `agentic`, `autonomous`, `tool-use` | Priorité haute |
| LLM | `LLM`, `language model`, `transformer`, `GPT`, `Claude` | Priorité haute |
| Sandbox | `sandbox`, `isolation`, `container`, `microVM` | Priorité moyenne |
| RAG | `RAG`, `retrieval augmented`, `GraphRAG`, `knowledge graph` | Priorité haute |
| MCP | `MCP`, `model context protocol` | Priorité haute |
| Harness | `code agent`, `IDE`, `coding assistant` | Priorité moyenne |
| Benchmark | `benchmark`, `evaluation`, `leaderboard` | Priorité moyenne |

## Format de sortie

Chaque paper détecté génère une entrée dans `content/veille/arxiv-feed.qmd` :

```markdown
### [Titre du paper]
- **arXiv** : 2507.17311
- **Date** : 2025-07-23
- **Auteurs** : Intern-AI et al.
- **Catégorie** : cs.AI
- **Tags** : agentic, climate, research
- **Résumé** : Premier self-evolving AI agent pour climate science...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2507.17311
```

## Derniers papers détectés

::: callout-note
## Section auto-générée
Cette section est automatiquement mise à jour par le script `scripts/veille_arxiv.py` via GitHub Actions. Les entrées ci-dessous sont générées à chaque exécution.
:::

<!-- ARXIV_FEED_START -->
### LLMs and Agentic AI Systems for Smart Grids: A Tutorial on Architectures and Applications
- **arXiv** : 2607.18147v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-20
- **Auteurs** : Daniela Rojas, Abdulwahab Albassam, Aidan G. Leung, Jett Ngo, Ryan Luo
- **Score** : 12
- **Résumé** : Large language models (LLMs) and agentic AI systems have evolved from natural language tasks to using external tools to plan, retrieve, and act in technical domains. In smart grids, recent work applies agentic schemes to forecasting, optimization, and control, wrapping trusted solvers behind languag...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.18147v1

### Do Language Models Dream of Binding Molecules? Benchmarking LLMs under Spatial Constraints
- **arXiv** : 2607.18144v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-20
- **Auteurs** : Thomas MacDougall, Maksim Kuznetsov, Roman Schutski, Rim Shayakhmetov, Maxim Malkov
- **Score** : 10
- **Résumé** : Structure-based drug design (SBDD) leverages the 3D structure of protein targets, often complemented by other spatial constraints, to generate candidate binding molecules. While diffusion models have dominated as a leading paradigm for high-quality 3D molecule generation, LLM-based methods are rapid...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.18144v1

### SGA: Plug&amp;Play Geometric Verification for Educational Video Synthesis
- **arXiv** : 2607.18116v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-20
- **Auteurs** : Lopez Jhon, Hinojosa Carlos, Ghanem Bernard
- **Score** : 9
- **Résumé** : Recent work leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) to generate executable code for pedagogical animations using libraries such as Manim. However, ensuring spatial correctness and visual legibility remains challenging, as existing frameworks emphasize pedagogical content while overlooking geometric o...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.18116v1

### WorldCupArena: Fine-Grained Evaluation of Language Models and Deep-Research Agents on Football Forecasting
- **arXiv** : 2607.18084v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-20
- **Auteurs** : Zhaokai Wang, Tianlin Gui, Jiayuan Rao, Shangzhe Di, Yihong Tang
- **Score** : 8
- **Résumé** : Predicting a football match before kickoff requires more than knowing past results: a model must use changing information and make a clear prediction before the answer is available. We present WorldCupArena, a dynamic benchmark for language models and deep-research agents. The 2026 FIFA World Cup is...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.18084v1

### It's Not What You Say, It's How You Say It: Evaluating LLM Responses to Expressions of Belief
- **arXiv** : 2607.18232v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-20
- **Auteurs** : Kevin Du, Clara Kümpel, Michelle Wastl, Alex Warstadt
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : Users frequently express their beliefs to large language models (LLMs). In some situations, the LLM should accept these contextual beliefs as true. In others, they should stick to their prior knowledge. Notably, users' expressions of belief (EoBs) can take linguistically diverse forms - using presup...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.18232v1

### SWE-Pruner Pro: The Coder LLM Already Knows What to Prune
- **arXiv** : 2607.18213v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-20
- **Auteurs** : Yuhang Wang, Yuling Shi, Shaoqiu Zhang, Jialiang Liang, Shilin He
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : Pruning long context for coding agents has been a vital technology for efficient context management. While existing context pruning methods such as SWE-Pruner realize this by attaching a separate code classifier, we find the agent itself encodes internal representations indicating the relevance of c...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.18213v1

### TRIM: Reducing AI-Generated CodeSlop via Agent Trajectory Minimization
- **arXiv** : 2607.18161v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-20
- **Auteurs** : Alex Mathai, Shobini Iyer, Aleksandr Nogikh, Petros Maniatis, Franjo Ivancic
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : Coding agents are increasingly used to accelerate code generation in many downstream tasks, such as fixing bugs, building applications, and prototyping. However, despite their value as coding assistants, agent-generated code tends to be larger and more verbose than the corresponding human-written im...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.18161v1

### Testing Retrieval-Augmented Generation Systems with Chunk Coverage
- **arXiv** : 2607.18155v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-20
- **Auteurs** : Jinhan Kim, Samuele Pasini, Paolo Tonella
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)-based systemsootnote{For brevity, RAG-based systems are referred to as RAG systems throughout this paper.} are increasingly deployed in high-stakes settings where correct behaviour depends not only on the language model but also on the retrieval component that ...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.18155v1

### FinSAgent: Corpus-Aligned Multi-Agent RAG Framework for Evidence-Grounded SEC Filing Question Answering
- **arXiv** : 2607.18102v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-20
- **Auteurs** : Jijun Chi, Zhenghan Tai, Hanwei Wu, Tung Sum Thomas Kwok, Hailin He
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : Financial question answering over U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) filings requires retrieving and synthesizing heterogeneous evidence dispersed across long, standardized, and highly redundant disclosures. Existing retrieval-augmented and multi-agent systems typically derive retrieval q...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.18102v1

### Can We Break LLMs Out of Self-Loops? Fine-Grained Reasoning Control with Activation Steering
- **arXiv** : 2607.18100v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-20
- **Auteurs** : Sheldon Yu, Tong Yu, Xunyi Jiang, Rohan Surana, Gagan Mundada
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : Extended reasoning has become standard for frontier Large Language Models (LLMs), yet the trajectories these models produce remain largely uncontrollable. Existing methods for shaping how a model reasons are prompt based approaches and operate at the input level, offering no fine-grained control ove...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.18100v1

### VDAR-Router: Adaptive LLMs Routing via Verbalized Query Difficulty Analysis Retrieval
- **arXiv** : 2607.18098v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-20
- **Auteurs** : Yu-Chien Tang, Jun-Chen Hung, Wen-Chih Peng, An-Zi Yen
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : Large language models are increasingly used in practical systems, making efficient model selection important for reducing deployment cost. LLM routing has emerged as a practical solution for allocating each input query to an appropriate model under a desired cost-performance trade-off. Existing rout...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.18098v1

### Judge-dependent safety gains and model-specific helpfulness costs of evidence-sufficiency prompting in clinical LLMs
- **arXiv** : 2607.18086v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-20
- **Auteurs** : Koyar Afrasyab
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : Background: LLM judges increasingly score whether clinical language models give overconfident answers under incomplete evidence, yet whether a measured "safety gain" reflects real behavior change or the judge's calibration is unresolved. Using a structured evidence-sufficiency prompt as a test case,...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.18086v1

### SelectInfer: Selective Neuron Loading and Computation for On-Device LLMs
- **arXiv** : 2607.18081v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-20
- **Auteurs** : Huzaifa Shaaban Kabakibo, Eric Schniedermeyer, Artem Burchanow, Lin Wang
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities across a range of Natural Language Processing (NLP) tasks, but their high computational and memory demands pose significant challenges for deployment on resource-constrained edge devices. Existing approaches to model compression ...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.18081v1

### Sparse Evidence Can Suffice: Agentic Evidence Seeking for Multimodal Video Misinformation Detection
- **arXiv** : 2607.18080v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-20
- **Auteurs** : Haochen Zhao, Yongxiu Xu, Xinkui Lin, Dong Xie, Jiarui Lu
- **Score** : 6
- **Résumé** : Multimodal video misinformation detection is commonly formulated as a holistic video-understanding task, where the entire video and its associated content are processed and judged in a single pass. However, real-world misinformation often exhibits a sparse and compositional evidence structure: a rel...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.18080v1

### The Label Complexity of Class-Conditional Coverage under Distribution Shift
- **arXiv** : 2607.18088v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-20
- **Auteurs** : Weijia Han, Lisha Qu
- **Score** : 5
- **Résumé** : Standard evaluation of many recognition systems contains distribution shift by construction, since benchmarks place disjoint conditions in the training and test splits. Under such a shift, split conformal prediction keeps marginal coverage near the nominal level while per-class coverage fails silent...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.18088v1

### Vector Search As Nearest Neighbor Matching: RAG-based Policy Learning in Causal Inference
- **arXiv** : 2607.18225v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-20
- **Auteurs** : Masahiro Kato, Taka Kato
- **Score** : 4
- **Résumé** : We propose one-step and two-step methods for policy learning with retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). We formulate RAG-based action selection under the potential outcome framework. In the two-step method, vector search retrieves action-specific neighboring evidence in an embedding space, the gener...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.18225v1

### VEHBench: A Stage-Local Diagnostic Benchmark for LLM-Assisted Vibration Energy Harvester Design
- **arXiv** : 2607.18181v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-20
- **Auteurs** : Depeng Su, Yuyu Luo, Guobiao Hu
- **Score** : 4
- **Résumé** : Battery-free Internet of Things (IoT) requires iterative design of vibration energy harvesters (VEHs) under coupled physical constraints, while LLMs are emerging as interface layers for engineering workflows. However, existing engineering benchmarks primarily assess final artifact validity, offering...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.18181v1

### OR Else: A Differentiable Trust Region for Policy Optimization
- **arXiv** : 2607.18163v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-20
- **Auteurs** : Chinmay Rane, Kanishka Tyagi, Michael Manry
- **Score** : 4
- **Résumé** : PPO and the GRPO baseline studied here use clipped surrogate objectives whose favorable-direction saturation introduces an abrupt change in the scalar objective's derivative. We ask whether Output Reset (OR), a smooth one-sided saturation rule, offers a useful alternative for large language model po...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.18163v1

### LLM-as-a-Coach: Experiential Learning for Non-Verifiable Tasks
- **arXiv** : 2607.18110v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-20
- **Auteurs** : Tianzhu Ye, Li Dong, Guanheng Chen, He Zhu, Xun Wu
- **Score** : 4
- **Résumé** : Reinforcement learning (RL) on open-ended tasks compresses an LLM's rubric-based evaluation into a scalar reward, discarding rich textual feedback and conflating responses with distinct quality profiles. We propose Experiential Learning (EL), which repurposes the feedback model from an LLM-as-a-Judg...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.18110v1

### Automated Discovery Has No Universally Superior Harness
- **arXiv** : 2607.18235v1
- **Date** : 2026-07-20
- **Auteurs** : Akshat Gupta, Jermaine Lei, Alexander Lu, Gopala Anumanchipalli, Leshem Choshen
- **Score** : 3
- **Résumé** : Autonomous discovery systems such as OpenEvolve and TTT-Discover are often used as general-purpose harnesses. However, in practice these are composite systems combining several design choices about archives, parent selection, exploration, and budget allocation into a single recipe. Because discovery...
- **Lien** : https://arxiv.org/abs/2607.18235v1

<!-- ARXIV_FEED_END -->
